a: Error values the framework returns for specific insights within the overall evaluation.
An error that indicates that there was a failure in generating the insight.
An error that indicate the requested insight isn’t available.
An error that indicates that the version you requested isn’t available on this device and OS combination.
An error that indicates that the insight version you requested isn’t available.
An error that indicates that the app has reached the rate limit for the particular insight type.
The insight result that an evaluation request returns.
Values describing the usage of insight evaluation.
A value that indicates the app couldn’t use the insights because of a technical failure, they arrived too late to be of use, or other issue.
A value that indicates the app canceled the operation, so no decision required.
A value that indicates the app used the insights for evaluation of their usefulness or for model training with no impact on a decision process.
A value that indicates the app evaluated the insights and they were a factor in adding checks or blocking the action.
A value that indicates the app evaluated the insights and they were a factor in making the operation easier for the user.
A value that indicates the app evaluated the insights, but the insights didn’t have any impact on the decision or user flow.
A class that defines data and methods the framework uses to perform evaluations.
Values that indicate the status of the app’s authorization to request evaluations.
A value that indicates a person has authorized this app to request evaluations.
A value that indicates a person denied permission to use the framework.
A value that indicates a person has previously denied authorization to use the framework, but the app can present a request again at a later time.
A person hasn’t yet consented to allow this app to request evaluations.
A value that indicates insights aren’t available on the current device.
Errors the framework can return if there are errors processing an evaluation request.
An error that indicates the framework couldn’t produce an evaluation for the provided context.
An error that indicates evaluations are unavailable due to a person’s options.
An error that indicates the payload signature for the evaluation result from the server was invalid.
An error that indicates the framework couldn’t complete the evaluation.
An error that indicates evaluations are not available on this device.
An error that indicates the framework has received too many requests.
An error that indicates the framework couldn’t complete the evaluation due to server or network issues.
A structure that provides details about the evaluations to request and provides information the framework requires for the evaluation to take place.
A structure you use to make a request for a specific insight.
A protocol that insight evaluation types conform to.
Values that define the required model version of the insight.
A value that defines the latest version and includes adjustments as fraud patterns change.
A value that defines a specific model version intended to be run in parallel with the latest version.
Values that represent the types of operation it’s possible to request evaluations for.
A value that indicates an account operation including registration, login, or the modification of account details.
A value that indicates a communication operation.
A value that represents action types that don’t fall into other available categories.
A value that indicates some form of payment or purchase.
A value that indicates the use of some resource.
An associated type that represents an insight.
An insight to request to examine indications that someone may be actively coaching a person to perform actions.
An enumeration of insight values present in the available schema versions.
Values available in schema version1.
The levels of trust the framework returns for the “is likely being coached” insight.
A result that indicates there’s a high risk that a third party is coaching a person in some form.
A result that indicates there’s a medium chance that a third party is coaching a person in some form.
A result that indicates a lack of evidence as to whether or not coaching is occurring.
A value that represents one of the available schema versions for the particular insight.
Evaluate transactions for potential coercive activity while preserving people’s privacy.
A protocol that describes the trust insight model and the associated evaluation properties.
The result type for this particular insight which will typically be an enumeration value.
Additional information about the evaluation.
Returns an authorization status that indicates whether a person permitted the app to request evaluations for the given context.
The timestamp that indicates when the framework created the on-device portion of the assessment, in UTC.
Creates a new insight evaluator object you use to request insights.
Initializes a new insight request with the provided insight type, schema, and model versions.
Creates the context with which to request insights.
Result values and errors for the requested insights.
The identifier for this particular result.
The insight ID.
The identifier for the type of insight requested.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the framework created the insight with the newest available model version.
The model version used for this particular insight.
The model version the framework used for this insight.
The requested model version.
The newest model that could be requested.
The newest model that’s available to request.
The type of operation you’re requesting the evaluation for.
Result value from a request for this insight.
The result value from a request for this insight.
Reports the consumption status, and optionally provides one or more associated insight identifiers.
Reports the consumption status, and optionally provide one or more associated insights.
Creates an insight request for the given insight type.
Requests authorization from a person to generate evaluations.
Requests the evaluation of insights.
An app defined identifier.
An optional identifier you can use to tie an assessment to a specific transaction.
The insight you’re requesting.
An integer that indicates the schema version the framework should use to perform the evaluation.
The requested schema version.
The server-side timestamp that indicates when the server processed the evaluation, in UTC.
A signed data object that contains details about the insight.
The identifier string for the type of insight this represents.
